title: Computer Graphics
description: Introduction to computer graphics covering 2D and 3D rendering, geometric transformations, lighting models, and GPU programming. Students study the graphics pipeline, coordinate transformations, rasterization, texture mapping, ray tracing, and shader programming using OpenGL/WebGL. MATH220 (Linear Algebra) is essential for understanding geometric transformations, projections, and lighting computations. CS201 provides the data structures knowledge (trees, spatial data structures) needed for scene management and rendering optimization.
topics: Scene graphs and spatial data structures
outcomes: Implement lighting models and texture mapping
Understand GPU architecture and parallel rendering
